Warranty & Guarantee

We stand by the quality of our products with industry-leading protection.

 5-Year Limited Warranty on brass components and finish integrity (excluding normal patina development).

Covered for repair or replacement of defective parts (labor & installation excluded).

 Visual defects must be reported within 15 days of delivery with photos and order proof.

Need help? Email contact@insideast.com with your order number.

 Note: Unlacquered brass is designed to patinate this natural aging is not a defect, but a celebrated hallmark of authenticity.

Frequently Asked Questions

 What is Unlacquered Brass? 

Unlacquered brass is a raw, living finish that is not sealed. Over time, it will oxidize and develop a unique, warm patina that gives it an authentic antique look. It interacts with the environment, so it will age beautifully.

 How do I maintain the finish? 

If you love the natural patina (darkening), no maintenance is required—simply wipe it with a soft cloth. If you prefer it shiny, you can easily restore the original luster using a brass polish like Brasso.

 Is this fixture solid brass? 

 Yes. This fixture is crafted from solid brass, ensuring exceptional durability and resistance to corrosion, unlike plated alternatives.

 Are replacement parts available? 

Yes, the traditional construction means parts are accessible and replaceable, ensuring your fixture can be maintained for a lifetime.

A hand-finished brass heirloom crafted for performance, designed to age with beauty, and built to outlast trends.

 ✨ One-of-a-Kind Craft: Each dome is hand-hammered, no two are identical. Reserve yours before it's crafted.

Authentic Hand-Forged Elegance

Every Insideast flush mount light is a testament to the skill of the artisan. Using centuries-old hammering techniques, we transform raw copper into a living piece of functional art for your hallway, bedroom, or low-ceiling spaces.

A Legacy in Every Strike

Forged from high-purity solid copper, this flush mount light radiates timeless warmth. Each piece is unique, bearing the individual marks of its maker. No two lights are ever identical.

Designed as a "Living Finish," the unlacquered surface develops a deep, rich patina over time, telling the story of your home through its evolving character. Perfect for low-ceiling spaces where traditional pendants won't fit.

Artisan Appreciation

★★★★★

"Perfect solution for my low ceilings! The hammered texture adds so much character."

Michael Chen

★★★★★

"The patina development is beautiful. It looks like an antique piece already."

Sarah Johnson

★★★★★

"Installation was straightforward and the quality is exceptional. Worth every penny."

David Wilson

Questions & Support

 What is "Unlacquered Copper"? ▼ 

It is raw copper that hasn't been chemically sealed. This allows the metal to react with oxygen, creating a unique antique patina that deepens with time.

 Is this suitable for low ceilings? ▼ 

Yes! This flush mount design is specifically created for rooms with limited ceiling height, providing ample illumination while maximizing headroom.

 How do I restore the original shine? ▼ 

If you prefer the bright look, you can easily restore it using a copper cleaner or a lemon-and-salt paste once or twice a year.

 Is professional installation required? ▼ 

We recommend a licensed electrician for installation to ensure safety and proper electrical connection, though the included bracket makes it relatively straightforward.

 Can I use LED bulbs? ▼ 

Yes! The fixture is compatible with standard E26/E27 LED bulbs, which are energy-efficient and produce minimal heat.

Frequently Asked Questions

 Is this a complete system with valves? 

This is a Trim Kit . It includes all the external brass components shown (Gooseneck Arm, Head, Handheld, Tub Spout, 3 Handles). You will need to install standard rough-in valves (1/2" NPT) behind the wall—typically 2 valves for hot/cold mixing and 1 diverter, matching the 3-handle layout.

 Can I customize the shower head size? 

 Yes. As noted by our customers (see David H.'s review), we can customize the shower head size for a small fee if you need something larger than the standard size. Please contact us before ordering.

 What is the reach of the tub spout? 

The tub spout has a generous reach of 8.38 inches , making it suitable for most built-in and freestanding bathtubs. It is crafted from solid brass to match the rest of the system perfectly.

 How high is the gooseneck arch? 

The gooseneck arm rises approximately 6 inches above the water outlet before curving down. This extra height is perfect for taller users or for creating a more spacious, luxurious showering experience.

We ship via express courier with secure packaging. As Kerri and Gilda mentioned in their reviews, shipping is fast, punctual, and precise.

Frequently Asked Questions

 Can this system be used with a handheld sprayer? 

This specific model is a **2-outlet system** designed for overhead showering and tub filling only. If you require a handheld sprayer, please check our 3-outlet gooseneck system or contact us for a custom configuration.

 Is the rough-in valve included? 

No, this package is a **Trim Kit**. You will need to purchase standard 1/2" NPT rough-in valves tailored to your local plumbing codes (typically one mixing valve and one diverter, or independent valves depending on your preference).

 Can I customize the spout or arm length? 

 Yes. As noted in our reviews (see David H.), we offer customization for a small fee. We can extend the shower arm reach or adjust the tub spout projection to perfectly fit your bathtub setup. Please contact support before ordering.

 What is the tub spout reach? 

The tub spout extends 8.38 inches from the wall surface to the aerator center, providing ample clearance for most standard and freestanding bathtubs.

 How fast is shipping? 

We ship via express courier. Customers like Kerri, Gilda, and Dany confirm in their reviews that items arrive "promptly," "very fast," and "punctual and precise."

Your Questions, Answered

 What’s the difference between “smooth hammered” and “heavy hammered”? ▼ 

Smooth hammered uses lighter, more frequent strikes for gentle undulation — ideal for modern or transitional spaces. Heavy hammered has deeper dimples and rugged texture, suited for rustic or industrial settings.

 Can I use dimmable LED bulbs? ▼ 

Yes — as long as your dimmer switch is LED-compatible. The open design ensures excellent heat dissipation for long bulb life.

 Is the bracket included? How thick is the wall mount? ▼ 

Yes, a low-profile concealed bracket is included. It sits only 1.2 inches from the wall, keeping the sconce sleek and shadow-free.

 How do I clean or maintain the brass? ▼ 

Dust with a soft, dry cloth. Avoid chemical cleaners. If desired, buff occasionally with a soft cotton cloth to maintain shine — or let it age beautifully untouched.

Questions & Support

 How is the cone shaped? ▼ 

Each cone is hand-spun on a lathe and seamed using traditional brass-working techniques — no welding or casting. This ensures strength and a clean, continuous silhouette.

 Does the brushed finish scratch easily? ▼ 

No; the brushing is deep and mechanical, not superficial. Minor marks blend into the texture and patina over time, adding character rather than damage.

 Can I adjust the cord length after installation? ▼ 

Yes; the fabric-wrapped cord is fully adjustable at the canopy. Extra length can be neatly coiled and hidden inside.

 Is it dimmable? ▼ 

Absolutely, compatible with all standard dimmer switches when used with dimmable bulbs (LED, incandescent, or halogen).

 What does “oxidized copper” mean? ▼

Artisan Excellence in Every Detail

Moroccan Craftsmanship

Forged and finished by master artisans in Fez using centuries-old brass casting and chasing techniques each piece is one-of-a-kind.

Swiveling Square Spout

8″ reach with full 360° rotation ideal for deep farmhouse sinks. The square profile adds architectural elegance and superior water control.

US-Standard Plumbing

Moroccan Craftsmanship

Forged and finished by master artisans in Fez using centuries-old brass casting traditions. Every seam, curve, and detail is shaped by hand no two faucets are identical, just as no two homes tell the same story.

A Bridge to Tradition

The classic bridge faucet traces its roots to early 20th-century kitchens where function and form coexisted in pure harmony. This reinterpretation honors that legacy with a clean square spout, elegant 8″ spacing, and hand-chased details cast in solid brass using sand-molding refined over generations.

Engineered for Daily Joy

The unlacquered surface begins luminous and golden, then deepens into a rich, characterful patina a living finish that evolves with your home. Whether in a restored bungalow or new farmhouse kitchen, it becomes a daily heirloom not just a fixture.

Important Note for Plumbers

Not all plumbers accept handcrafted faucets. We recommend consulting yours before purchase. Contact us for guidance or installation support we’ll help you find a compatible professional.

Lifetime Expert Support

Frequently Asked Questions

 What is unlacquered brass and how does it patina? 

Unlacquered brass is raw, untreated brass—no protective coating. Over time, it naturally oxidizes, developing a warm, darker patina influenced by water minerals, humidity, and use. Some prefer the bright gold look (polish to restore); others love the antique bronze tone it becomes. This evolution is intentional and celebrated.

 What’s the difference between the four handle styles? 

All are solid brass and age identically. Simple Cross: classic raised cross with subtle relief. Flat Cross: minimalist, flush-profile cross. Lever: smooth, ergonomic lever for modern ease. Butterfly: ornate vintage wings—dramatic and decorative. Choose based on aesthetic preference and ergonomics.

 Will this fit my sink/countertop? 

Designed for standard 3-hole sinks with 8″ center-to-center spacing (common in US kitchens). You’ll need 3 holes: left (hot), center (spout), right (cold). Faceplate is included to cover existing 4-hole sinks if needed. Verify hole spacing and countertop thickness (max 1.5″) before ordering.

 Do I need a plumber? What if mine refuses? 

We recommend a licensed plumber familiar with non-standard fixtures. Some plumbers resist handcrafted pieces due to unfamiliarity—but we provide detailed guides and are happy to consult directly with them. Contact us before purchase for pre-installation support.

 Can I order custom finishes or sizes? 

Yes! We offer oil-rubbed bronze, antique brass, and bespoke sizing (e.g., 10″ bridge, taller spout). Matching soap dispensers, pot fillers, and bathroom sets also available. Just email us with your vision—we love collaboration.

 Why choose brushed over polished brass? ▼

 Can I adjust the cord length after installation? ▼

 Is it dimmable? ▼

The 10” is ideal for smaller islands or single pendants. The 12” makes a bolder statement perfect for large dining tables or open-concept spaces where scale matters.

Frequently Asked Questions

 What is Unlacquered Brass? 

Unlacquered brass is a raw, living finish that is not sealed. Over time, it will oxidize and develop a unique, warm patina that gives it an authentic antique look. It interacts with the environment, so it will age beautifully.

 How do I maintain the finish? 

If you love the natural patina (darkening), no maintenance is required—simply wipe it with a soft cloth. If you prefer it shiny, you can easily restore the original luster using a brass polish like Brasso.

 Is this fixture solid brass? 

 Yes. This fixture is crafted from solid brass, ensuring exceptional durability and resistance to corrosion, unlike plated alternatives.

 Are replacement parts available? 

Yes, the traditional construction means parts are accessible and replaceable, ensuring your fixture can be maintained for a lifetime.

Questions & Support

 How do I clean unlacquered brass? ▼ 

Simply wipe with a dry or slightly damp cloth. Avoid chemical cleaners or polishing — they strip
 the developing patina. Let time enhance its character naturally.

 Will fingerprints show? ▼

 finish experience.

 Can I slow down the patina? ▼ 

You can apply a light coat of mineral oil occasionally to delay oxidation, but we recommend
 embracing the natural aging process for authentic character.

 Is it dimmable? ▼

 incandescent, or halogen).

Frequently Asked Questions

 Is copper hard to maintain? 

Not at all. Unlacquered copper is low maintenance if you allow it to patina naturally. Simply rinse after use and wipe dry avoiding hard water spots. If you prefer the bright shiny look, you can polish it, but most clients love the antique living finish.

 Does the tub get cold quickly? 

Copper is an excellent heat conductor. It absorbs the heat from the water and radiates it back, keeping your bath water warmer for significantly longer than cast iron or acrylic tubs.

 What is included with the tub? 

The tub comes as a standalone soaking basin. Overflow kits and drains are sold separately or can be selected in the configuration options above.

 What cleaning products should I avoid? 

Avoid abrasive cleaners, steel wool, or harsh chemicals like bleach. These can strip the patina or scratch the copper. Mild soap and a soft cloth are all you need for daily cleaning.

 Can I use bath salts or essential oils? 

Yes, you can! However, rinse the tub thoroughly after use. Acidic ingredients (like lemon or vinegar) in some bath products may brighten spots on the copper, but these will blend back into the patina over time.

 How is the tub shipped? 

The tub is securely crated in a wooden box to ensure it arrives in perfect condition. We offer free insured shipping worldwide, and the crate is designed to protect against any transit impacts.

Frequently Asked Questions

 Is this a complete system with the valve? 

 Yes. This package includes the exposed mixing valve (hot and cold handles), the riser pipe, and the showerhead. You do not need to purchase a separate rough-in valve.

 Can I add a handheld sprayer or tub spout later? 

This specific model is designed as a minimalist 1-outlet system (showerhead only). If you need a handheld sprayer or tub filler, please check our "3-Function" or "Diverter" models, as this valve body is dedicated to a single overhead outlet.

 What are the installation requirements? 

Detailed in Image 2: accurate rough-in centers of 5.9" (150mm) are ideal, but the included S-Unions (Z connectors) allow for adjustment between approx 5.5" and 6.5". Connections are standard 1/2" NPT.

 Will the finish change? 

Yes, this is Unlacquered Brass . It arrives shiny but will naturally darken and patina over time due to oxidation. This "living finish" is intentional and highly sought after for its organic, vintage character.

 Is the showerhead swappable? 

Yes, the shower arm terminates in a standard 1/2" ball joint or thread. You can easily unscrew the included Rose head and replace it with a Square or Round head (sold separately or selected in configuration) if you wish to change the look later.

Frequently Asked Questions

 Is copper hard to maintain? 

Not at all. Unlacquered copper is low maintenance if you allow it to patina naturally. Simply rinse after use and wipe dry avoiding hard water spots. If you prefer the bright shiny look, you can polish it, but most clients love the antique living finish.

 Does the tub get cold quickly? 

Copper is an excellent heat conductor. It absorbs the heat from the water and radiates it back, keeping your bath water warmer for significantly longer than cast iron or acrylic tubs.

 What is included with the tub? 

The tub comes as a standalone soaking basin. Overflow kits and drains are sold separately or can be selected in the configuration options above.

 What cleaning products should I avoid? 

Avoid abrasive cleaners, steel wool, or harsh chemicals like bleach. These can strip the patina or scratch the copper. Mild soap and a soft cloth are all you need for daily cleaning.

 Can I use bath salts or essential oils? 

Yes, you can! However, rinse the tub thoroughly after use. Acidic ingredients (like lemon or vinegar) in some bath products may brighten spots on the copper, but these will blend back into the patina over time.

 How is the tub shipped? 

The tub is securely crated in a wooden box to ensure it arrives in perfect condition. We offer free insured shipping worldwide, and the crate is designed to protect against any transit impacts.

Smooth quarter-turn operation, drip-free for life. Lab-tested for 500,000+ cycles engineered to outlast mass-market alternatives.

Elegant Wall-Mounted Design

Frees up precious counter space while adding architectural elegance. The high-arc gooseneck spout provides generous clearance for pots and oversized cookware perfect for modern kitchens and vintage-inspired bathrooms alike.

A Finish That Ages With Dignity

Unlike lacquered brass that chips and fades, unlacquered brass evolves. Each faucet darkens and deepens uniquely reacting to water, air, and daily use. The result? A rich, antique bronze patina that becomes more beautiful year after year.

100% Solid Brass — No Compromises

From spout to handle, every component is cast in pure solid brass no zinc cores, no thin plating. This means exceptional durability, substantial heft, and resistance to corrosion. Built to outlast trends and generations.

Lifetime Installation Support

Common Questions

 Do I need to buy a rough-in valve separately? 

Yes. This faucet is sold without a rough-in valve—a standard requirement for wall-mounted faucets. You’ll need a compatible 1/2" ceramic-disc rough-in valve (available at most plumbing suppliers). We’re happy to recommend models upon request.

 Will this work with my existing sink? 

Absolutely. Wall-mount faucets work with virtually any sink farmhouse, undermount, top-mount, or even vessel sinks. Just ensure your wall framing and plumbing are prepared for wall-mount installation.

 How do I maintain the unlacquered finish? 

Let it patina naturally for that authentic vintage look. If you prefer the bright brass appearance, gently polish with a brass cleaner or a paste of lemon juice + baking soda. The finish will continue to evolve this is intentional and celebrated.

 Is it suitable for bathroom use? 

Yes! Its elegant profile and compact footprint make it ideal for wall-mounted bathroom sinks, vanities, or even outdoor kitchens.

 What’s your return policy? 

We offer a no-questions-asked 60-day return window. If it’s not right for your space, return it in original condition for a full refund. Returns must be unused and in original packaging.

Frequently Asked Questions

 What is unlacquered brass and how does it patina? 

Unlacquered brass is raw brass without protective coating. Over time, exposure to air and moisture
 forms a warm, darker patina unique to your environment and usage. This evolution is intentional and
 enhances character.

 How do I care for my brass sink? 

Rinse after use and wipe dry. For cleaning, use mild soap and water. Avoid abrasive cleaners or
 acidic substances. Occasional polishing with brass-specific products can maintain shine—or embrace
 the natural patina.

 Can I install this as an undermount sink? 

Yes. This sink is designed specifically for undermount installation, ensuring a seamless countertop
 surface and easy cleaning.

 Is this sink suitable for outdoor use? 

Yes—solid brass is highly resistant to corrosion and weathering. The patina actually protects the
 metal over time. Ideal for outdoor kitchens or bars.

 Do you offer custom sizes or shapes? 

Yes. We offer full customization—including non-square shapes, special depths, and unique finishes.

Why This Faucet Is Worth The Investment

Living Unlacquered Finish

Industry-leading ceramic disc cartridges deliver smooth, drip-free performance with exceptional durability. Lab-tested for 500,000+ cycles that's decades of daily use.

Moroccan Artisan Heritage

Each faucet is meticulously handcrafted by skilled Moroccan artisans who have perfected their craft over generations. Using traditional metalworking techniques, they shape solid brass into this elegant, compact design that brings authentic character and warmth to your bathroom.

A Finish That Tells Your Story

The unlacquered brass finish is designed to age gracefully, developing a unique patina that reflects your home's story. Each mark and subtle color shift adds character, creating a one-of-a-kind piece that grows more beautiful with use. This living finish celebrates the natural beauty of brass rather than fighting against it.

Precision Engineering Meets Artisan Craft

Beneath the artisan exterior lies serious engineering. Premium ceramic cartridges ensure smooth, drip-free operation for years. The compact spout provides perfect clearance for small sinks, while the solid brass construction guarantees structural integrity and lasting performance that stands up to daily use.

Professional-Quality Installation Made Easy

Frequently Asked Questions

 Is this faucet worth the investment compared to cheaper alternatives? 

Absolutely. While mass-produced faucets may cost less upfront, they typically use brass-plated zinc that degrades within 3-5 years. Our solid brass construction lasts 20+ years, making it more cost-effective long-term. Plus, you get the living patina finish, artisan craftsmanship, and a 5-year warranty. Most customers tell us it's the best bathroom upgrade they've made.

 How does the unlacquered brass patina develop? 

Unlacquered brass naturally oxidizes over time, developing a rich, darker patina. The rate depends on water chemistry, humidity, and use patterns. Each faucet develops its own unique character—some prefer the warm golden look when new, others love the antique bronze that emerges over months.

 Can I clean or polish the brass to restore the original finish? 

Yes! Use a brass polish or a mixture of lemon juice and baking soda to restore the bright brass finish. However, the unlacquered nature means it will continue to patina. Many customers love the lived-in look, but you can maintain the golden shine with regular polishing.

 Is this compatible with my existing plumbing? 

The faucet is designed for standard 3/8" NPT plumbing connections (USA). It requires one pre-drilled hole. Most standard bathroom sinks accommodate this configuration, but please verify your sink specifications before ordering.

 What's included in the package? 

You'll receive everything needed for installation: the spout assembly, two handles, ceramic cartridges, supply hoses with correct regional fittings, mounting hardware, aerator assembly, and a detailed installation manual. Professional installation support is also available if needed.

 How long does shipping take? 

Delivery typically takes 5-7 days. Each faucet is handcrafted to order in Morocco by our skilled artisans. We'll keep you updated throughout the process with tracking information.

 What if I'm not satisfied with my purchase? 

We offer a 60-day home trial. If you're not completely satisfied, return it for a full refund. We also provide a 5-year warranty on fixtures and 1-year on parts, plus lifetime customer support. Your satisfaction is our top priority.

 How quickly does the patina develop? ▼

 How does the unlacquered brass finish age?

All components are fully compatible with U.S. plumbing standards, including 1/2" NPT threading for hassle-free installation and future maintenance.

Freestanding Tub Filler with Floor Mount Base

Handheld Shower Head with Holder

Premium Ceramic Cartridges (Pre-installed)

All Necessary Mounting Hardware

Comprehensive Installation Manual

Hot/Cold Water Supply Hoses

Brass Finish Care Instructions

Frequently Asked Questions

 What is unlacquered brass and how does it age? 

Unlacquered brass is raw brass without any protective coating. Over time, it develops a natural patina—a darkened, aged appearance that adds character and uniqueness to each piece. This living finish changes based on use, humidity, and handling, making your tub filler truly one-of-a-kind. If you prefer the bright, polished look, you can easily maintain it with brass polish.

 Is this tub filler compatible with my existing plumbing? 

Yes! This freestanding tub filler features standard 1/2" NPT threading, making it compatible with U.S. plumbing systems. The package includes all necessary supply hoses and mounting hardware. We recommend consulting with a professional plumber if you're unsure about your specific setup.

 How difficult is the installation process? 

Installation is straightforward for those comfortable with basic plumbing tasks. The tub filler comes with a detailed installation manual with step-by-step instructions. Most DIY enthusiasts can complete the installation in 2-3 hours. However, we always recommend hiring a licensed plumber to ensure proper installation and compliance with local codes.

 Will this match other brass fixtures in my bathroom? 

Absolutely! This tub filler is designed to coordinate beautifully with other unlacquered brass fixtures from the Insideast collection. Since unlacquered brass develops a natural patina, all your brass fixtures will age together, creating a cohesive and authentic vintage aesthetic throughout your bathroom.

 What's your return policy? 

We offer a 30-day return policy on all our products. If you're not completely satisfied with your purchase, you can return it within 30 days for a full refund. The item must be unused and in its original packaging. Please note that return shipping costs are the responsibility of the customer unless the item is defective or damaged.

Warranty & Guarantee

We stand by the quality of our products.

 5-Year Limited Warranty on plumbing and accessories for residential use.

 1-Year Warranty on wearable parts (cartridges, hoses, O-rings, cables, etc.).

Covered for repair or replacement in case of defects (installation/labor costs excluded).

 Visual damages must be reported within 15 days of delivery with photos and proof of purchase.

Questions or a claim? Email contact@insideast.com with your order number.

Questions & Support

 What is "Unlacquered Copper"? ▼ 

It is raw copper that hasn't been chemically sealed. This allows the metal to react with oxygen, creating a unique antique patina that deepens with time.

 How do I restore the original shine? ▼ 

If you prefer the bright look, you can easily restore it using a copper cleaner or a lemon-and-salt paste once or twice a year.

 Is professional installation required? ▼ 

We always recommend a licensed electrician for ceiling installations to ensure safety and proper electrical connection.

 Can I use high-wattage LED bulbs? ▼ 

Yes! The fixture supports up to 300W, making it perfect for bright Edison-style LEDs or high-power bulbs.

 Do you offer custom cord materials? ▼ 

Yes, we can provide different cord styles and materials upon request to match your specific interior design.

Why This Faucet Is Worth The Investment

Each faucet is meticulously crafted by skilled metalworkers using traditional techniques passed down through generations. You're not just buying a faucet—you're preserving an ancient craft and bringing authentic artisan quality to your home.

Solid Brass Construction (Not Plated)

Built entirely from solid brass—no hollow components, no thin plating that wears away. The substantial weight and durability you can feel immediately. This is a fixture designed to last generations, not just years.

Living Unlacquered Finish

Ages naturally over time, developing a rich patina that tells your home's unique story. Unlike lacquered finishes that chip and peel, unlacquered brass becomes more beautiful with age—celebrating the natural oxidation process rather than fighting it.

Traditional Construction—Easy Maintenance

Moroccan Artisan Heritage

Each faucet is meticulously handcrafted by skilled Moroccan artisans who have perfected their craft over generations. Using traditional metalworking techniques, they shape solid brass into this elegant wall-mount design that brings authentic character and timeless warmth to your bathroom.

This living finish celebrates the natural beauty of brass rather than fighting against it.

Precision Engineering Meets Artisan Craft

Beneath the artisan exterior lies serious engineering. Premium 1/2" ceramic disc cartridges ensure smooth, drip-free operation for years. The traditional construction with replaceable components means you can maintain this fixture for generations, combining old-world craftsmanship with modern reliability.

Frequently Asked Questions

 Is this faucet worth the investment compared to cheaper alternatives? 

Absolutely. While mass-produced faucets may cost less upfront, they typically use brass-plated zinc that degrades within 3-5 years. Our solid brass construction with traditional, repairable design lasts 20+ years, making it more cost-effective long-term. Plus, you get the living patina finish, artisan craftsmanship, easy maintenance, and a 5-year warranty. Most customers tell us it's the best bathroom upgrade they've made.

 How does the unlacquered brass patina develop? 

Unlacquered brass naturally oxidizes over time, developing a rich, darker patina. The rate depends on water chemistry, humidity, and use patterns. Each faucet develops its own unique character—some prefer the warm golden look when new, others love the antique bronze that emerges over months. It's a living finish that tells your home's story.

 Can I clean or polish the brass to restore the original finish? 

Yes! Use a brass polish or a mixture of lemon juice and baking soda to restore the bright brass finish. However, the unlacquered nature means it will continue to patina naturally. Many customers love the lived-in look, but you can maintain the golden shine with regular polishing if you prefer.

 What does "traditional construction" mean for maintenance? 

Traditional construction means the faucet is built with accessible, replaceable components—not sealed units that can't be serviced. Standard 1/2" ceramic cartridges, washers, and O-rings can be easily replaced with common tools, ensuring you can maintain this fixture for decades without expensive specialized service calls. It's designed to be repaired, not replaced.

 Is this compatible with my existing plumbing? 

The faucet uses 1/2" BSP (British Standard Pipe) connections, which are standard internationally. It requires wall-mount installation with a concealed rough-in valve system. We recommend having a professional plumber verify compatibility with your specific setup and handle the installation to ensure proper function and warranty coverage.

 What's included in the package? 

You'll receive everything needed for installation: the wall-mount spout assembly, two cross handles, standard 1/2" ceramic disc cartridges, built-in valve system, wall mounting plates, aerator assembly, all installation hardware, and a detailed technical manual with diagrams. Professional installation support is also available if needed.

 How long does shipping take? 

Delivery typically takes 8-10 days. Each faucet is handcrafted to order in Morocco by our skilled artisans. We'll keep you updated throughout the process with tracking information so you know exactly when to expect your fixture.

 What if I'm not satisfied with my purchase? 

We offer a 60-day home trial. If you're not completely satisfied, return it for a full refund—no questions asked. We also provide a 5-year warranty on fixtures and 1-year on wearable parts, plus lifetime customer support. Your satisfaction is our top priority.

 Can this faucet be customized? 

Yes! We offer customization options upon request. Whether you need that just contact usand we will provide you with all details.

 5-Year Limited Warranty on brass components and finish integrity (excluding normal patina development).

Covered for repair or replacement of defective parts (labor & installation excluded).

 Note: Unlacquered brass is designed to patinate—this natural aging is not a defect, but a celebrated hallmark of authenticity.

Frequently Asked Questions

 Does this system include the mixing valve? 

No, this is an exposed trim kit designed to connect to your existing water supply. It requires standard 1/2" NPT male-threaded outlets (hot and cold) coming from your wall. We recommend pairing it with a high-quality thermostatic or pressure-balance rough-in valve suitable for your local plumbing codes.

 How does the unlacquered brass finish age? 

Since the brass is raw and sealed only with a light wax, it interacts with oxygen and water to develop a "living finish." Over time, it will naturally darken and acquire a unique, mottled patina. This is a desired aesthetic feature of artisan brass. If you prefer a shiny look, you can polish it regularly with Brasso or a similar brass cleaner.

 Can I install this if my pipes are already tiled in? 

This system acts as a "bridge" style faucet. It mounts directly to two rough-in pipes (Hot & Cold) sticking out of the finished wall. It does not require breaking the tile unless your existing pipe centers (the distance between hot and cold pipes) are drastically different from the standard 6" to 8" spread. Our included S-unions allow for adjustment between approximately 6 inches and 9 inches.

 Is the handheld sprayer and hose included? 

Yes, the complete set includes the main showerhead, the exposed riser pipe, the curved tub filler spout, and a matching solid brass handheld sprayer with a flexible hose and wall-mount bracket.

 What is the water flow rate? 

The system is designed to provide a generous, rain-like flow. However, it is compatible with standard water restrictors if your local plumbing codes require a specific GPM (e.g., 1.8 or 2.5 GPM). The pressure has been tested up to 150 PSI to ensure durability.

 What is the warranty coverage? 

We offer a 5-Year Limited Warranty covering the structural integrity of the brass and mechanical operation (leaks, diverter failure). The natural patina process of unlacquered brass is not considered a defect; it is a characteristic of the material.

Frequently Asked Questions

 What’s the difference between this and the Straight Bridge model? 

The Verdeau features an elegant V-arch spout for added height and presence, while the straight bridge is more minimalist and low-profile. Both use identical solid brass construction and unlacquered finish—but the Verdeau makes a stronger architectural statement.

 Can I install the sprayer later if I start with “Faucet Only”? 

Yes! You can add the solid brass side sprayer anytime. Just ensure your sink has a fourth pre-drilled hole (or drill one). We sell the sprayer separately—or contact us for a discounted upgrade within 90 days.

 How do I clean or maintain unlacquered brass? 

Light cleaning: warm water + mild soap. For polishing: use a brass-specific polish or a paste of lemon juice + baking soda. But many owners embrace the patina—it tells your kitchen’s story. Note: avoid vinegar or harsh chemicals, which accelerate uneven aging.

 Will this match my existing unlacquered brass hardware? 

Absolutely. All Insideast unlacquered brass fixtures are made from the same alloy and hand-finished identically. They’ll age in harmony across your space—creating a cohesive, evolving patina story.

 Is professional installation recommended? 

DIY installation is straightforward for most with basic plumbing experience. The included manual guides you step-by-step, and our team offers live support. For peace of mind, many customers use a local plumber (~$150–$250)—but it’s not required.

Finish note: unlacquered brass develops a natural patina — this is intentional and not a defect.

Frequently Asked Questions

 What is unlacquered brass and how does it patina? 

Unlacquered brass is pure brass with no protective coating. Over time, it reacts with oxygen, moisture, and hand oils to develop a warm, mottled patina—deepening from bright gold to antique bronze. This aging is natural and unique to each piece.

 How do I care for my unlacquered brass faucet? 

Simply wipe with a soft cloth and mild soap. Avoid abrasive cleaners or polishes—they’ll strip the evolving patina. Patina can be slowed with occasional waxing (optional), but we recommend embracing its organic evolution.

 Will this fit my existing sink? 

Yes—this faucet is designed for standard single-hole installations. It uses a ½" BSP Male thread (British Standard Pipe), which is critical for plumbing compatibility.

 What’s the difference between the two handle styles? 

Both are solid brass and function identically. The Simple Cross has rounded arms for a classic vintage look; the Flat Cross features streamlined, squared arms for a more modern heritage aesthetic.

 Can I install this faucet myself? 

Absolutely. It includes all mounting hardware and fits standard plumbing. Most DIYers complete installation in under 30 minutes with basic tools (adjustable wrench, plumber’s tape). A full guide is included in the PDF download.

Frequently Asked Questions

 Is this faucet worth the investment compared to cheaper alternatives? 

Absolutely. While mass-produced faucets may cost less upfront, they typically use brass-plated zinc that degrades within 3-5 years. Our solid brass construction lasts 20+ years, making it more cost-effective long-term. Plus, you get the living patina finish, artisan craftsmanship, and a 5-year warranty. Most customers tell us it's the best kitchen upgrade they've made.

 How does the unlacquered brass patina develop? 

Unlacquered brass naturally oxidizes over time, developing a rich, darker patina. The rate depends on water chemistry, humidity, and use patterns. Each faucet develops its own unique character—some prefer the warm golden look when new, others love the antique bronze that emerges over months.

 Can I clean or polish the brass to restore the original finish? 

Yes! Use a brass polish or a mixture of lemon juice and baking soda to restore the bright brass finish. However, the unlacquered nature means it will continue to patina. Many customers love the lived-in look, but you can maintain the golden shine with regular polishing.

 Is this compatible with my existing plumbing? 

The faucet is designed for standard 1/2" NPT plumbing connections (USA) or 1/2" BSP (UK/Europe). It requires three pre-drilled holes with 8" center-to-center spacing. Most standard kitchen sinks accommodate this configuration, but please verify your sink specifications before ordering.

 What's included in the package? 

You'll receive everything needed for installation: the bridge spout assembly, two handles (your selected style), ceramic cartridges, 17" supply hoses with correct regional fittings, mounting hardware, aerator assembly, and a detailed installation manual. Professional installation support is also available if needed.

 How long does shipping take? 

Delivery typically takes 8-10 days. Each faucet is handcrafted to order in Morocco by our skilled artisans. We'll keep you updated throughout the process with tracking information.

 What if I'm not satisfied with my purchase? 

We offer a 60-day home trial. If you're not completely satisfied, return it for a full refund. We also provide a 5-year warranty on fixtures and 1-year on parts, plus lifetime customer support. Your satisfaction is our top priority.

Why the Bridgeford Faucet Is a Heirloom Investment

Refined Modern Proportions

The Bridgeford features a balanced silhouette lower profile than traditional bridges, with a gracefully curved spout that provides ample clearance without visual bulk. Designed for contemporary kitchens that value elegance and function.

Enhanced Engineering

Upgraded internal flow channels reduce turbulence for quieter operation. The swiveling spout rotates 180° smoothly, and the aerator is removable for easy cleaning engineered for decades of daily use.

Square Solid Brass Sprayer (Optional)

A first for artisan faucets: a matching square sprayer in solid unlacquered brass. No plastic parts. It integrates seamlessly with the faucet’s aesthetic and develops patina in harmony.

Precision-Machined Simple Cross Handles

The signature of the Bridgeford minimalist cross handles with subtle beveled edges. Machined from solid brass rod, not cast, for superior strength and refined detail.

Designed for Real Kitchens

The Bridgeford’s 8-inch spread and optimized spout height (6") fit most standard sinks. Whether your sink is farmhouse, undermount, or top-mount, the clean lines and unlacquered finish integrate effortlessly adding warmth without overwhelming.

Living Finish in Action

This customer installed their Bridgeford 6 months ago. Notice how the brass has deepened to a warm antique gold around the handles and base areas of frequent touch while the spout retains brighter highlights. This is the beauty of unlacquered brass: it tells your story.

No Compromises in Materials

From the solid brass body and handles to the brass sprayer head and even the mounting hardware every visible and functional part is solid brass. No zinc cores. No plastic internals. This is heirloom-grade construction.

Functional Artistry

The optional square sprayer isn’t just aesthetic it delivers a powerful, focused stream for cleaning and filling. The trigger is smooth and balanced, and the hose retracts cleanly into the base. A rare blend of form and function.

Detailed Installation Manual

Frequently Asked Questions

 How is the Bridgeford different from the original Bridge Faucet? 

The Bridgeford features a more refined base profile, slightly lower height, extended spout reach (9.00" vs 7.7"), and precision-machined simple cross handles with subtle beveling. The optional square sprayer is also exclusive to the Nova line. It’s an evolution—retaining the soul of the original but elevated for modern aesthetics.

 Is the square sprayer only available with the Bridgeford? 

Yes—for now. The square sprayer was designed specifically to complement the Bridgeford’s clean lines and proportions. It’s made from the same solid unlacquered brass and will patina in harmony with the faucet.

 What’s the weight of the Bridgeford faucet? 

The 8" King version weighs approximately 3.5 lbs—slightly heavier than the original due to the denser base casting. This substantial heft is a hallmark of true solid brass construction.

 Can I install this without the deck plate? 

Yes—the faucet is designed for both 1-hole (with deck plate) and 3-hole (8" spread) installations. The faceplate is included but optional. Just let us know your sink configuration at checkout if you prefer to omit it.

 Does the unlacquered finish require special care? 

No special care is needed—but the beauty is in the choice. Let it patina naturally for a unique, antique look. Or polish occasionally with brass cleaner (e.g., Brasso) to maintain the bright gold. Either way, it’s solid brass—no risk of peeling or flaking.

 What’s the water flow rate? 

Approx. 1.8 GPM at 60 PSI (standard for kitchen faucets). The aerator is removable for cleaning or higher flow if desired.

 Do you offer custom lead times or finishes? 

We offer unlacquered brass only (to ensure authenticity and longevity), but custom lead times are possible for large orders (e.g., whole-home or multi-unit). Contact us at support@insideast.com .
